mission.title: Install and Configure Keycloak on Kubernetes
mission.description: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management solution that enables you to add authentication to applications and secure services with minimal effort. Installing Keycloak allows you to manage user authentication and authorization seamlessly across your applications.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm repository to your Helm client to access the Keycloak chart.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Keycloak using Helm
steps.description:
Install Keycloak with the specified version and create a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install keycloak bitnami/keycloak --version 25.2.0 --namespace keycloak --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Keycloak pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace keycloak
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Keycloak deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment keycloak --namespace keycloak -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"keycloak","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Access Keycloak
steps.description:
Port-forward the Keycloak service to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/keycloak --namespace keycloak 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Keycloak release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall keycloak --namespace keycloak
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any custom resource definitions (CRDs) created by Keycloak.
```bash
kubectl delete crd keycloaks.keycloak.org
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Keycloak to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace keycloak
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your Keycloak configuration and data.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace keycloak -o yaml > keycloak-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Keycloak
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Keycloak to the latest version available in the repository.
```bash
helm upgrade keycloak bitnami/keycloak --version 25.2.0 --namespace keycloak
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Keycloak pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace keycloak
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If your Keycloak pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace keycloak
``` 
Common fixes include checking for misconfigurations in environment variables or resource limits.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access Keycloak, ensure the service is running and check the port-forward command.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace keycloak
``` 
Make sure you are forwarding the correct ports.

troubleshooting.title: Failed to connect to database
troubleshooting.description:
If Keycloak fails to connect to the database, verify the database configuration in the values file.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment keycloak --namespace keycloak
``` 
Ensure that the database service is running and accessible.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Keycloak will have all pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the Keycloak console via the specified port. You should also have resource limits configured and the service accessible.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
